fixes_applied.description: Added device-specific interaction logic for complimentary pages info icon. Replaced hover-only interaction with conditional logic: hover() for desktop (viewport width >= 768), click() for mobile/tablet (viewport width < 768). This matches the test case requirement for responsive device-specific interactions.

Added device-specific interaction logic for additional pages info icon. Replaced hover-only interaction with conditional logic: hover() for desktop (viewport width >= 768), click() for mobile/tablet (viewport width < 768). This matches the test case requirement for responsive device-specific interactions.
This violation was marked as a false positive in the scoring report. All expect() assertions in the script already have explicit timeout parameters. No fix was required.

Consolidated verbose logging in Precondition 1 from 7 separate framework_logger.info() calls to 1 comprehensive log line at completion. Removed intermediate logging statements for account creation, printer creation, enrollment start, printer selection, plan selection, and enrollment completion. Kept only the final comprehensive log with all key data (tenant_email, entity_id, sub_id). This follows the coding standard of keeping logging concise with 1 log line per step.
fixes_applied.original_code: Multiple framework_logger.info() calls throughout precondition setup
fixes_applied.corrected_code: Single comprehensive log: framework_logger.info(f"Precondition 1 completed: Printer enrolled with 50 pages plan - tenant_email={tenant_email}, entity_id={printer_data.entity_id}, sub_id={sub_id}")

post_fix_summary.expected_score_improvement: Score should increase from 88 to 100 (12 point improvement)
post_fix_summary.recommendation: Re-run scoring agent to verify all fixes applied correctly. Expected score: 100/100. All MODERATE violations (V001, V002) have been resolved by adding device-specific interaction logic. The MINOR violation (V004) has been resolved by consolidating verbose logging. V003 was confirmed as a false positive and required no action.
